original_hypothesis.title: Turnstile CSR registration during ASDOFT deployment can revert or mis-register rewards if the hardcoded Turnstile address differs across environments (deployment-time fragility)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ASDOFT constructor conditionally calls Turnstile.register on chainid 7700/7701 with a hardcoded Turnstile address. If this address is incorrect (or Turnstile behavior changes), deployment can revert or CSR rewards can be misdirected, impacting operations and funding assumptions tied to CSR.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a practical ops hazard: deployment-time external call with a fixed address and no error handling. It can cause failed deployments or silent economic misconfiguration.
deployment_assumptions: Turnstile contract is deployed at 0xEcf044C5B4b867CFda001101c617eCd347095B44 on both Canto mainnet and testnet
Turnstile.register does not revert and behaves consistently
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: asdOFT.sol hardcodes Turnstile address in constructor; no deployment script validation of Turnstile address/codehash.

inventory_refs.function: constructor(string memory _name, string memory _symbol, address _lzEndpoint, address _cNote, address _csrRecipient)

preconditions: Hardcoded Turnstile address is wrong or Turnstile.register reverts

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Constructor performs external call to hardcoded system contract without try/catch.
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Deployer-controlled parameter _csrRecipient impacts reward recipient correctness.

operational_questions: Is the Turnstile address correct on both mainnet/testnet and future forks?
Does Turnstile.register ever revert (e.g., already registered, invalid recipient)?
Is CSR registration required for protocol economics or optional?
validation_requirements: Verify Turnstile codehash at the hardcoded address on target chains
Dry-run deployment on fork with register call
Document fallback plan if CSR registration fails

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: If misconfigured, deployment fails or CSR is directed incorrectly.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Economic impact realized over time via lost CSR rewards.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Failed deployment or CSR revenue loss/misdirection.

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: CSR registry entries for ASDOFT contract; reward recipient addresses.

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
